item.title: Trump pausing Project Freedom for short period
item.description: President Trump announced Tuesday night that he's pausing Project Freedom for "a short period of time" to see if an agreement with Iran can be finalized.

item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire holds for now, Hegseth says, as Trump looks to finalize deal
item.description: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.

item.title: California accuses State Farm of delaying and underpaying wildfire claims
item.description: The California Department of Insurance is accusing State Farm, the nation's largest home insurance company, of delaying, denying and underpaying claims after last year's wildfires. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: In Lynette Hooker probe, Coast Guard seeks info about mystery sailboat
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is trying to find the owners of a sailboat that may have been moored next to Lynette Hooker's vessel the night she disappeared in the Bahamas, marking a new development in the search for the missing Michigan woman.

item.title: Former Georgia Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan slams 2020 election probe as "all about fear and intimidation"
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking the names of people who worked the 2020 election in Fulton County, Georgia. Former Georgia GOP Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan, who is running for governor as a Democrat, joins "The Takeout" with his reaction.

item.title: Trump pauses Project Freedom in Strait of Hormuz to see if Iran deal can be struck
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he has paused Project Freedom, a U.S. effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Coast Guard seeks public's help in finding missing wife in Bahamas
item.description: The Coast Guard is asking for the public's help in the search for a Michigan woman who disappeared last month in the Bahamas.

item.title: Trump argues rising gas prices are worth war with Iran
item.description: Iran's leverage depends on President Trump feeling political pressure at home. Gas prices are up and support for the war is not. Ed O'Keefe reports on domestic pressure on the Trump administration.

item.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
item.description: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and sickened at least four others, officials said.

item.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
item.description: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.

item.title: Dramatic new video shows United plane striking pole, truck while landing in Newark
item.description: A United Airlines jet clipped a delivery truck as it came in for landing on Sunday in Newark, New Jersey. It happened on one of the busiest stretches of highway in the U.S. Kris Van Cleave has a new look.

item.title: What we know about the cruise hantavirus outbreak and its origins
item.description: A rare form of hantavirus is believed to have spread person-to-person on board a cruise ship. Three people are dead, four more are ill and nearly 150 others are stranded aboard the ship, where they will stay potentially for months until they are cleared to leave. Tom Hanson has more.

item.title: Manhunt on for retired special forces soldier accused of shooting wife
item.description: Authorities say Craig Berry, a retired special forces soldier, shot his wife and then fled into the woods in northern Tennessee.

item.title: Here's why U.S. debt is surpassing GDP for the first time since World War II
item.description: For the first time since World War II, the U.S. national debt has surpassed the size of its economy. CBS News MoneyWatch associate managing editor Aimee Picchi has more.

item.title: Tom Homan says Trump administration is using "smarter enforcement" in Minneapolis
item.description: ICE and Border Patrol have come under intense public scrutiny over their immigration enforcement tactics, particularly in Minneapolis. Following the shooting deaths of two U.S. citizens by immigration agents in January, the Trump administration sent Border Czar Tom Homan to work with local officials. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez sat down exclusively with Homan.

item.title: Homan: "Things weren't perfect" in Minneapolis, but ICE not backing down
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan conceded "things weren't perfect" during the crackdown in Minneapolis, but stressed that the administration is not backing down from its mass deportation effort.

item.title: Trump backing 7 Indiana Republicans looking to oust GOP lawmakers who voted against gerrymandering
item.description: Republicans are keeping a close eye on Ohio and Indiana on Tuesday as voters cast their ballots in state and congressional primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez has all the key details.

item.title: Radio flashback: CBS says farewell to Studio 9 in 1964
item.description: Studio 9 was where the voices that made CBS News Radio famous came together to knit the world through reportage over the airwaves. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces a tribute to the space made ahead of its closure in the summer of 1964.

item.title: Katie Porter pokes fun at viral staffer video in new campaign ad
item.description: Katie Porter's campaign for California governor got off to a rough start last year after a video surfaced of her telling a staffer to "get out of my f---ing shot" while she was recording a video. The former Democratic congresswoman is now using that in a new campaign ad. Political strategists Meaghan Mobbs and Matt Bennett join with analysis.

item.title: Extended interview: Tom Homan says ICE embraced "smarter" deportation tactics after Minneapolis
item.description: CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez speaks with border czar Tom Homan about the immigration crackdown in Minneapolis, changes ICE has made since then and more.

item.title: Rep. Johnny Olszewski proposes bill for 18-year Supreme Court term limits
item.description: Recent polling released by NBC News shows that only 22% of Americans have confidence in the U.S. Supreme Court. In the wake of that growing unpopularity, a new proposal in Congress would amend the Constitution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices. Democratic Rep. Johnny Olszewski of Maryland joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Border czar Tom Homan says Trump administration isn't backing down on mass deportations
item.description: White House border czar Tom Homan sat down for an exclusive interview with CBS News immigration and politics correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez on Tuesday to defend the Trump administration's deportation efforts. Montoya-Galvez joins "The Takeout" to preview the interview.

item.title: Full briefing: Marco Rubio says Iran leadership is "insane in the brain"
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters on Tuesday with an update about the war with Iran. He said, "We are only responding if attacked first," and that Operation Epic Fury is over.

item.title: To avoid risk of mines, Navy directs ships on path farther from Iran
item.description: The U.S. warns that transiting the normal route could be "extremely hazardous" because of mines laid in the Strait of Hormuz by Iran.

item.title: Meta trained its AI on copyrighted work, new lawsuit alleges
item.description: The lawsuit claims that Meta's Llama is generating summaries — and, in some cases, verbatim copies — of original works.

item.title: Iran war will worsen food insecurity in already fragile nations, humanitarian group warns
item.description: The aid organization Mercy Corps is warning that the Iran war will have devastating consequences for nations already on the brink of a humanitarian crisis. Kate Phillips-Barrasso, vice president for global policy and advocacy at Mercy Corps, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Rubio says Iran war has shifted to new phase: "Project Freedom"
item.description: On Tuesday, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the initial phase of the Iran war, dubbed "Operation Epic Fury," is over. He described this latest phase as "Project Freedom." CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: Tennessee, Alabama consider congressional redistricting in special sessions
item.description: The nationwide partisan congressional redistricting push has come to Tennessee and Alabama. CBS News' Nicole Valdes and Shawna Mizelle report.

item.title: Delta ending food and beverage service on shorter flights
item.description: Routes that are under a certain distance will no longer offer food and beverage service, the airline said.

item.title: Utz potato chips sold nationwide recalled over salmonella risk
item.description: Utz Quality Foods is recalling some of its Zapp's and Dirty brand potato chips because an ingredient may be contaminated by salmonella.
